tasks.title: Bell state with Sampler
tasks.description: Create a Bell state circuit and sample it with the primitive stack.
tasks.prompt: Write Qiskit code that builds a 2-qubit Bell state circuit, measures all qubits, and executes it using the recommended Sampler primitive for the requested SDK version.

expected_signals.success_hint: uses a Bell circuit and a primitives execution path

tasks.title: Optimization-aware transpilation
tasks.description: Transpile a simple circuit for a fake backend with a chosen optimization level.
tasks.prompt: Write Qiskit code that creates a 3-qubit GHZ-style circuit and transpiles it for a fake backend at optimization level 2, printing the circuit depth after transpilation.

expected_signals.success_hint: imports transpile and a fake backend

tasks.title: Parameterized circuit with Estimator
tasks.description: Evaluate a parameterized ansatz and observable with Estimator.
tasks.prompt: Write Qiskit code that defines a one-parameter ansatz on one qubit, binds the parameter to pi/4, and estimates the expectation value of Z using the recommended Estimator primitive for the target version.

tasks.title: Statevector expectation value
tasks.description: Compute a Pauli expectation value from a statevector.
tasks.prompt: Write Qiskit code that prepares a single-qubit |+> state and computes the expectation value of Pauli X using Statevector utilities, then prints the numeric result.

expected_signals.success_hint: uses Statevector and Pauli/X operator APIs

tasks.title: TwoLocal ansatz construction
tasks.description: Build a compact variational ansatz with a standard library circuit class.
tasks.prompt: Write Qiskit code that constructs a 3-qubit TwoLocal ansatz with ry rotations and cz entanglement and prints the number of parameters.

expected_signals.success_hint: imports TwoLocal from the circuit library

tasks.title: SparsePauliOp observable grouping
tasks.description: Create a multi-term observable with SparsePauliOp.
tasks.prompt: Write Qiskit code that constructs a SparsePauliOp for the terms ZI and IZ with coefficients 0.5 and 0.5, then prints the operator.

tasks.title: Measure active qubits
tasks.description: Create a circuit with idle qubits and measure only active ones when requested.
tasks.prompt: Write Qiskit code that creates a 3-qubit circuit, applies operations only on qubits 0 and 2, then measures only the active qubits into a matching classical register.

expected_signals.success_hint: uses explicit classical registers or measure instructions

tasks.title: Preset pass manager
tasks.description: Use a generated preset pass manager for a simple circuit.
tasks.prompt: Write Qiskit code that creates a simple 2-qubit circuit and runs it through a preset pass manager for optimization level 1, then prints the transformed circuit depth.

expected_signals.success_hint: uses generate_preset_pass_manager or the current equivalent

tasks.title: Text circuit drawing
tasks.description: Render a small circuit without requiring GUI support.
tasks.prompt: Write Qiskit code that creates a 2-qubit circuit with an H and CX gate and prints a text diagram of the circuit so the example remains offline-friendly.

expected_signals.success_hint: uses circuit.draw with text output or prints the circuit directly

tasks.title: Simple control-flow branch
tasks.description: Build a small circuit using if_test style control flow.
tasks.prompt: Write Qiskit code that measures one qubit and conditionally applies an X gate to a second qubit using the current structured control-flow API such as if_test.

expected_signals.success_hint: uses a structured control-flow context manager

tasks.title: QASM round-trip
tasks.description: Export and re-import a simple circuit using supported QASM helpers.
tasks.prompt: Write Qiskit code that creates a Bell circuit, exports it to an OpenQASM string using the version-appropriate helper, reconstructs the circuit from that string, and prints the resulting depth.

expected_signals.success_hint: uses qasm2/qasm3 helpers or the current recommended import/export API

tasks.title: BackendSampler result counts
tasks.description: Sample a measured circuit and inspect quasi/count-style output.
tasks.prompt: Write Qiskit code that builds a measured Bell circuit, runs it with BackendSampler or the current equivalent local primitive, and prints the returned quasi distribution or counts-like object.

expected_signals.success_hint: uses BackendSampler or the replacement primitive interface
